title: Day 3 · File + Web tools
subtitle: A compact map of Day 3's core changes: ToolContext, fs_safety, local discovery tools, and the web-tool boundary.

diagrams.label: Main loop
diagrams.title: From prompt to file Observation
diagrams.summary: The model proposes tool_use; ToolRegistry executes through ToolContext, which carries cwd, SkipPolicy, and ReadFileState, then tool_result blocks return to messages.

nodes.label: user prompt
nodes.detail: The user still writes natural language. CLI parses the prompt and --cwd, but it does not decide which file to read.

nodes.label: messages
nodes.detail: messages stores the user question, assistant tool_use, and harness-returned user tool_result blocks.

nodes.label: AnthropicProvider
nodes.detail: The provider remains a protocol adapter: it sends tool schemas to the model and translates tool_use content blocks into internal ToolCall objects.

nodes.label: ModelResponse
nodes.detail: The model returns requests, such as list_files followed by read_file pyproject.toml. It does not access the filesystem directly.

nodes.label: ToolRegistry
nodes.detail: ToolRegistry routes by ToolCall.name and passes the same ToolContext into the Python tool function.

nodes.label: ToolContext
nodes.detail: This is Day 3's key abstraction: tools do not read global state; they receive cwd, skip rules, and read_state through ToolContext.

nodes.label: local file tools
nodes.detail: Local tools perform the real directory and file reads, filename search, and content search. Each one obeys cwd and skip policy.

nodes.label: tool_result blocks
nodes.detail: Day 3 packs every tool_result for the same assistant turn into one immediately following user message, matching Anthropic's expected shape.

nodes.label: stdout
nodes.detail: The terminal only renders trace: tool_call, observation, and final. Full messages and read_state stay inside the harness.

diagrams.label: fs_safety gate
diagrams.title: The fs_safety gate inside read_file
diagrams.summary: read_file does not read the disk immediately. It first locks the path to cwd, checks text and size, records ReadFileState, and truncates the output.

nodes.label: read_file
nodes.detail: The model only supplies a relative path, such as pyproject.toml. That argument cannot go straight to Path.read_text.

nodes.label: resolve_in_cwd
nodes.detail: resolve_in_cwd resolves the model's path, then proves it is still under cwd with relative_to(cwd).

nodes.label: ensure_text_file
nodes.detail: Known text suffixes pass immediately; other files are checked for NUL bytes in the first 1 KiB.

nodes.label: ensure_within_size
nodes.detail: Files over 256 KiB are rejected for whole-file reads, nudging the model toward grep or a later offset/limit version.

nodes.label: Path.read_text
nodes.detail: The harness reads from disk only after all three gates pass.

nodes.label: ReadFileState
nodes.detail: Day 3 only records this data. Day 4 read-before-edit will use it to detect whether the file changed after the model read it.

nodes.label: truncate_output
nodes.detail: Tool observations are truncated before they enter model context, so one read cannot flood the conversation.

nodes.label: ToolResult
nodes.detail: Success and failure both become ToolResult. Missing files, escaping paths, binary files, and huge files do not crash the Agent Loop.

diagrams.label: Local tools
diagrams.title: Local discovery tools share cwd and skip rules
diagrams.summary: list_files, glob, grep, and project_tree answer different discovery questions, but they all share cwd, SkipPolicy, and .gitignore filtering through ToolContext.

nodes.label: ToolContext
nodes.detail: ToolContext is the shared entry point for tools. Later permission, session, and memory state can hang off this same object.

nodes.label: cwd
nodes.detail: cwd is the boundary for local file tools. Every relative path must prove it stays inside cwd.

nodes.label: SkipPolicy
nodes.detail: SkipPolicy filters .git/, .venv/, node_modules/, __pycache__/, and the cwd root .gitignore before tool output reaches the model.

nodes.label: local file tools
nodes.detail: These tools let the model see code, but the model is still not accessing the filesystem directly. Execution stays in the harness.

nodes.label: list_files
nodes.detail: list_files reads one directory level, which is useful for a first pass over the project root.

nodes.label: glob
nodes.detail: glob finds files by name pattern and returns the most recently modified matches first.

nodes.label: grep
nodes.detail: grep prefers ripgrep and falls back to pure Python, while keeping output in path:line:content form.

nodes.label: project_tree
nodes.detail: project_tree gives a compact project overview with depth, node-count, and output-length caps.

nodes.label: Observation
nodes.detail: Every local discovery tool eventually returns a bounded observation inside the next user tool_result content blocks.

diagrams.label: Web boundary
diagrams.title: Web tools still execute in the harness
diagrams.summary: web_fetch and web_search let the Agent see the outside world, but URL validation, HTTP requests, HTML conversion, and truncation all happen inside the tool boundary.

nodes.label: tool_use
nodes.detail: The model can only ask for a URL fetch or a query search. The harness tool performs the network access.

nodes.label: ToolRegistry
nodes.detail: ToolRegistry routes web_fetch / web_search to the matching function and passes ToolContext.

nodes.label: web boundary
nodes.detail: The web boundary centralizes URL scheme, credentials, hostname, timeout, content-type, response-size, and output-length checks.

nodes.label: web_fetch
nodes.detail: web_fetch allows only http(s), rejects credential-bearing URLs, and checks URL length and hostname shape.

nodes.label: web_search
nodes.detail: web_search uses DuckDuckGo HTML as the teaching fallback and unwraps redirect URLs into real result URLs.

nodes.label: external HTTP
nodes.detail: httpx.Client performs the real request with User-Agent, follow_redirects, and timeout; HTTP errors become observations.

nodes.label: normalize
nodes.detail: HTML pages become markdown; search results become title + URL lines, giving the model clean text.

nodes.label: truncate_output
nodes.detail: Pages and search results can be long, so the final observation is truncated before it enters messages.

nodes.label: ToolResult
nodes.detail: Successful content, network errors, and unsupported content-type failures all return as ToolResult.
